purpose: Algorithmic and mathematical frontier map for making Kolm's compiler, distillation, quantization, runtime, evaluation, privacy, registry, device, and agent surfaces stronger than generic wrappers or fine-tuning dashboards.

sources.lesson: Approximate second-order sensitivity lets post-training quantization choose safer rounding decisions.
Activation-aware salient weight preservation makes low-bit quantization more robust for LLMs.
Activation outliers can be migrated into weights so weight-and-activation quantization becomes more hardware-friendly.
Orthogonal rotations can reduce outliers and make 4-bit inference safer without changing model function in full precision.
Incoherence processing and lattice codebooks improve extreme low-bit quantization.
Additive codebooks are a serious path for sub-3-bit model compression when normal scalar quantization collapses.
KV cache quantization can be asymmetric and tuning-free, reducing long-context memory pressure.
Long-context inference is dominated by KV memory, so KV quantization must be a first-class runtime policy.
IO-aware tiling matters as much as arithmetic count for exact attention.
Parallelism and work partitioning decide realized attention throughput on GPU.
Paged KV memory management turns serving into an operating-systems scheduling problem.
Prefix reuse and structured generation need runtime-level scheduling, not only prompt engineering.
Multiple decoding heads can draft several tokens in parallel and verify them with tree attention.
Feature-level speculative sampling can reduce autoregressive latency while preserving target-model distribution.
Tree-based speculative inference needs parallel verification and explicit acceptance accounting.
Multi-level IRs and dialects reduce the cost of supporting many compiler targets.
Operator fusion, memory latency hiding, and hardware mapping need automated schedule search.
Production compiler UX needs explicit tensor program abstractions and meta-schedule/autotuning hooks.
Deployment configurations must connect input IR, target backend, runtime driver, and binary artifact.
Execution providers are the practical abstraction boundary for choosing CPU, CUDA, TensorRT, OpenVINO, DirectML, CoreML, QNN, and WebGPU-like targets.
Reverse KL can be better than forward KL for generative students because it avoids over-covering low-probability teacher regions.
Autoregressive distillation must address student-generated distribution mismatch.
Rationales can make smaller students more data-efficient than label-only fine-tuning or task distillation.
Preference optimization can be expressed as a stable direct classification-style objective rather than a full RL loop.
Distribution-free calibration can attach finite-sample risk control to model outputs under exchangeability.
Doubly robust off-policy estimators reduce variance and bias when replaying policy changes from logged data.
AI traces need standard span attributes for provider, model, usage, tool calls, and content-safety controls.
Per-example gradient clipping and calibrated Gaussian noise make private training auditable.
Renyi differential privacy gives a composable accounting language for repeated randomized mechanisms.
Private training needs developer-facing diagnostics around clipping, accumulation, accountant state, and optimizer invariants.
Local updates plus server averaging reduce communication pressure in decentralized training.
Federated learning needs aggregation protocols that hide individual client updates.
Byzantine-tolerant aggregation requires explicit assumptions about worker count and adversary budget.
Public artifact trust should include transparency-log-backed signing in addition to local offline verification.

math_primitives.objective: Minimize blockwise reconstruction error using an approximate inverse-Hessian sensitivity matrix.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Score per-layer rounding risk before emitting quantized .kolm payloads.

math_primitives.objective: Protect channels whose activation-weight product dominates output error.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Choose which channels get scale protection for extraction, classifier, and tool-call artifacts.

math_primitives.objective: Reduce activation outliers through smoothing or function-preserving rotations before low-bit lowering.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Generate target-specific transforms for W8A8, W4A4, and mobile/NPU runtime paths.

math_primitives.objective: Represent weights with incoherent lattice or additive codebook assignments under a memory budget.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Offer extreme-compression mode only when task calibration proves safe.

math_primitives.objective: Minimize KV cache bytes per generated token subject to long-context quality loss and runtime support.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Make long-context artifact receipts include KV cache policy and observed context window.

math_primitives.objective: Reduce HBM traffic and improve parallel work partitioning for exact attention kernels.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Decide whether a runtime target can meet p95 latency without changing model quality.

math_primitives.objective: Allocate KV blocks and reusable prefixes to maximize accepted throughput under memory pressure.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Plan vLLM/SGLang/TGI deployment manifests from workload trace shape.

math_primitives.objective: Draft token trees cheaply and verify acceptance with the target model distribution.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Attach lossless speculative decoding policy and acceptance receipts to runtime plans.

math_primitives.objective: Preserve semantic contracts while lowering through dialects to device-specific runtime payloads.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Make every .kolm target prove its input dialect, lowering path, runtime driver, and verifier probe.

math_primitives.objective: Search schedules, fusion decisions, memory layouts, and tensorization choices against measured target latency.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Compile target-specific kernels only when an autotune trace and fallback exist.

math_primitives.objective: Minimize reverse-KL or on-policy divergence so the student avoids low-probability teacher regions and deployment mismatch.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Select distillation losses by task, teacher access, and student capacity.

math_primitives.objective: Combine rationale supervision with direct preference losses when labels alone under-specify behavior.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Build label-next queues that ask for rationales, pairwise preferences, or hard labels based on expected K-score lift.

math_primitives.objective: Calibrate pass/fail or abstention thresholds to a finite-sample risk target.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Turn K-score from a scalar into a risk-controlled quality gate with abstention zones.

math_primitives.objective: Estimate policy or artifact lift from logged traces using behavior propensities and outcome models.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Let customers evaluate a new routing/artifact policy before exposing users to it.

math_primitives.objective: Compose privacy loss across training steps and exported aggregates while enforcing clipping and noise invariants.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Make differential privacy settings auditable instead of a checkbox.

math_primitives.objective: Aggregate client updates under communication, privacy, dropout, and Byzantine-adversary constraints.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Upgrade federated learning from foundation state to a proof-carrying enterprise workflow.

math_primitives.objective: Bind executable artifacts, tool calls, traces, and signatures into a verifiable supply chain.
math_primitives.kolm_use: Make compiled MCP/agent tools auditable by registry, enterprise admin, and offline verifier.

inventions.name: Kolm-Q Lagrangian Quantizer
inventions.objective: minimize E_holdout[loss(q(theta), x)] + lambda_mem bytes(q) + lambda_lat p95(q,target) + lambda_risk max(0, k_floor - k_score(q))
inventions.invariant: No quantized artifact is promoted unless held-out K-score, row hashes, model hash, calibration hash, and runtime target are all recorded.
build_steps: Add a quantization objective builder that accepts quality floor, memory ceiling, latency target, target runtime, and model family.
Score candidate methods using second-order block risk, activation saliency, outlier transform feasibility, and codebook availability.
Run calibration fixtures and record calibration hash before proposing a winner.
Emit fallback candidates with explicit rejection reasons for unsupported targets.
Write quantization receipts with method, bits, transforms, candidate scores, and holdout deltas.
Expose dry-run and JSON modes in CLI, API, account, and TUI surfaces.
acceptance_tests: A CUDA 7B extraction task selects AWQ/GPTQ only when K-score degradation stays under threshold.
A mobile QNN target rejects unsupported SmoothQuant/FP8 paths and returns ONNX/QNN-safe alternatives.
An extreme 2-bit request returns AQLM/QuIP only with explicit external-toolchain and benchmark-data caveats.
Receipts include calibration hash, model hash, target runtime, and all rejected candidates.

inventions.name: KV Context Compressor
inventions.objective: minimize bytes_per_token(K,V,context) subject to acceptance_quality >= floor and p95 <= target_ms
inventions.invariant: KV cache policy is runtime-scoped; the compiler cannot mark it portable unless the target engine supports equivalent cache layout and quantization.
build_steps: Add KV cache policy descriptors for uncompressed, int8, int4, int2, paged, and prefix-reuse modes.
Score context workloads by prompt length, generated length, prefix reuse, batch size, and memory ceiling.
Attach runtime support metadata for vLLM, SGLang, TGI, TensorRT-LLM, llama.cpp, and browser targets.
Emit receipts with context length, KV method, page size, prefix reuse policy, and observed fallback.
Add warnings when long-context claims rely on a runtime that is not the selected deployment target.
Expose a dry-run planner for account, CLI, and TUI runtime planning.
acceptance_tests: A long-context workload selects KV compression only when quality risk stays below configured loss.
A browser target rejects unsupported KV cache modes and offers a cloud fallback.
A repeated-prefix workload recommends SGLang/prefix cache or vLLM paged memory with reason codes.
Inference receipts include context length and KV cache policy.

inventions.name: Lossless Speculative Runtime
inventions.objective: maximize accepted_tokens_per_verify_step while preserving target_model_distribution and streaming order
inventions.invariant: Speculative decoding claims must record draft source, acceptance rate, rejected branches, and target-model verification.
build_steps: Define speculative policies for draft-model, Medusa-style heads, EAGLE-style feature draft, and disabled mode.
Add acceptance accounting to streaming and non-streaming completions.
Expose policy constraints for deterministic artifacts where speculative mode must be disabled.
Record accepted and rejected token tree statistics in runtime telemetry.
Fallback to normal decoding when acceptance rate drops below the configured floor.
Add receipt fields for draft method, verifier model, and acceptance proof.
acceptance_tests: Speculative mode does not change deterministic artifact outputs.
Streaming emits tokens in verified order only.
Low acceptance rate triggers fallback and emits diagnostic telemetry.
Receipts disclose draft method and target verification.

inventions.name: IR Autotune Compiler
inventions.invariant: Every compiled target must carry source IR hash, lowering pass list, target backend, runtime version, and fallback path.
build_steps: Create a Kolm behavior IR for schemas, prompts, tools, adapters, eval gates, and runtime constraints.
Add target descriptors with accepted input dialects, runtime payload formats, and verifier commands.
Implement lowering diagnostics for unsupported operators, tokenizers, modalities, and quantization methods.
Add measured or simulated schedule-search traces per target.
Record pass list and fallback target in each compiled artifact receipt.
Publish the matrix through docs, CLI, account, and OpenAPI.
acceptance_tests: Every artifact-runtime option has a lowering descriptor or an explicit unsupported diagnostic.
A target compile fails closed when tokenizer or modality is unsupported.
WASM and third-party runtime support remain package/external gates until proof exists.
Lowering receipts are stable and hash-addressed.

inventions.name: Reverse-KL Distill Forge
inventions.objective: choose argmin_loss in {reverse_kl, on_policy_gkd, rationale_ce, dpo_pairwise} under teacher_access, labels, preferences, and student_capacity
inventions.invariant: Teacher outputs, rationales, and preferences must preserve train/holdout separation and provenance before they can affect K-score.
build_steps: Add a loss planner that selects reverse KL, on-policy GKD, rationale supervision, pairwise preference, or no-train.
Estimate teacher-token cost and student capacity before starting generation.
Separate rationale rows from answer rows and preserve holdout-only metadata.
Add preference-pair builder from user corrections and evaluator disagreements.
Emit a distillation receipt with loss family, teacher model, row hashes, and holdout deltas.
Expose failure diagnostics when data is too small, noisy, or ambiguous.
acceptance_tests: A generation task with logits unavailable selects reverse-KL-compatible black-box/on-policy strategy only when supported.
A reasoning task asks for rationales or pairwise preferences instead of blind SFT.
Holdout-only rows never enter teacher generation or student training.
Distill receipts include teacher provenance and selected loss objective.

inventions.name: Conformal K-Score Calibrator
inventions.objective: calibrate threshold tau so empirical_miscoverage <= alpha, then estimate policy lift with doubly_robust(trace_log)
inventions.invariant: K-score confidence, abstention, and policy-lift claims must include calibration set hash and logged-policy metadata.
build_steps: Extend K-score output with calibrated confidence bands and abstention zones.
Record calibration set hash, alpha, threshold, and empirical coverage.
Add trace fields for behavior policy, candidate policy, outcome, and propensity where available.
Build a doubly robust replay estimator for routing/artifact changes.
Export replay and calibration artifacts to external eval systems.
Fail public claims when calibration evidence is absent or stale.
acceptance_tests: A low-confidence artifact returns abstain or collect-more-data rather than production-ready.
Calibration output includes alpha, calibration hash, threshold, and observed coverage.
Shadow evaluation reports confidence interval and missing propensity diagnostics.
OTEL exports do not leak raw content under zero-retention policy.

inventions.name: Private Federated Optimizer
inventions.objective: maximize utility(theta) subject to epsilon_total <= budget, delta <= target, secure_aggregation = verified, and tenant_isolation = true
inventions.invariant: Private/federated training cannot claim production privacy unless accountant state, clipping policy, secure aggregation state, and participant receipt hashes exist.
build_steps: Add DP accountant descriptors for sample-level and client-level privacy budgets.
Track clipping norm, noise multiplier, sampling rate, steps, epsilon, delta, and accountant method.
Connect DP accountant receipts to federated round receipts.
Require secure aggregation plugin verification before production claim scope changes.
Expose privacy-budget warnings in CLI, TUI, account, and API responses.
Keep live compliance certification external while local evidence packets improve.
acceptance_tests: A DP run without accountant metadata fails closed.
A federated run without secure aggregation cannot claim verified private aggregation.
Tenant RBAC blocks cross-tenant participant receipt access.
Compliance certification remains needs_live_certification without auditor evidence.

inventions.name: Byzantine Aggregation Receipts
inventions.objective: select aggregation rule a with valid_adversary_budget(f,n,d) and verifiable receipt chain for all accepted client updates
inventions.invariant: A robust aggregation receipt must state n, f, rule, client hashes, rejected clients, and assumptions; otherwise it is only foundation-state evidence.
build_steps: Extend aggregation receipts with adversary budget and supported-rule metadata.
Require rule-specific assumptions for FedAvg, coordinate median, trimmed mean, Krum, and secure aggregation.
Record rejected clients and reason codes.
Hash and sign aggregation round outputs.
Expose verifier diagnostics when assumptions are not satisfiable.
Link aggregation receipt hashes into compiled artifact manifests.
acceptance_tests: A Krum receipt with invalid f,n relationship fails verification.
A secure aggregation claim without plugin proof remains not_verified.
Rejected client updates are disclosed by hash and reason.
Compiled artifacts include aggregation lineage when trained federated.

inventions.name: Device Energy Scheduler
inventions.objective: minimize alpha*p95 + beta*memory + gamma*energy_per_call subject to offline_policy and K-score floor
inventions.invariant: Device runtime claims must include device class, memory budget, battery/energy estimate class, runtime version, and fallback target.
build_steps: Define device profiles for browser, iOS, Android, Apple Silicon, Windows NPU, Intel OpenVINO, Qualcomm QNN, and CPU-only.
Estimate memory and energy class from model size, quantization, context, and runtime.
Jointly select runtime, quantization, and KV cache policy.
Generate SDK and CLI integration snippets per target.
Keep mobile/WASM package claims behind package-release gates.
Record device profile in inference receipts.
acceptance_tests: A phone target chooses mobile-compatible runtime and does not claim unpublished SDK channel.
A browser target respects runtime-wasm package gate.
A CPU-only target offers a slower safe fallback with explicit latency warning.
Receipts include device class and fallback target.

inventions.name: Bandit Registry Ranker
inventions.objective: rank artifact a for user context c by posterior utility with penalties for missing proof, stale benchmark, unsupported runtime, and policy mismatch
inventions.invariant: Registry ranking must never outrank unverified artifacts above verified compatible artifacts solely because of downloads or engagement.
build_steps: Define ranking features from K-score, runtime compatibility, signature status, publisher verification, benchmark freshness, and usage outcomes.
Estimate offline lift using doubly robust replay from registry events.
Add policy filters for enterprise allowlists, license, compliance tags, and private registry scope.
Penalize stale proof and unsupported runtime targets.
Expose rank reasons in API, CLI, account, and registry pages.
Log decisions with OTEL attributes and receipt references.
acceptance_tests: An unsigned artifact cannot outrank a signed compatible artifact when quality is tied.
A private enterprise policy removes non-allowed publishers before ranking.
Rank reasons include proof, runtime, K-score, benchmark age, and policy match.
Offline evaluation reports uncertainty instead of claiming lift without logs.

inventions.name: Agent Proof-Carrying Tools
inventions.objective: compile tool behavior with schema risk <= alpha, token budget <= cap, signed dependencies, and traceable execution receipts
inventions.invariant: Compiled agent tools must bind schema, policy, dependency graph, allowed providers, and receipt verifier before registry publication.
build_steps: Generate MCP tool schemas from compiled artifact inputs and outputs.
Attach conformal or deterministic schema-risk gates to tool acceptance.
Sign dependencies and policy bundles.
Emit OTEL spans and execution receipts for each tool call.
Enforce token budget, allowed provider, and privacy policy per invocation.
Expose verifier output for Claude/OpenAI-compatible tool hosts.
acceptance_tests: A generated MCP tool includes schema, policy, signature, and verifier command.
Tool invocations exceeding token budget fail closed.
Registry publication rejects unsigned dependency graphs.
Execution receipts include provider, tool schema version, and artifact hash.

inventions.name: Cloud Cost-Latency Solver
inventions.objective: choose compute/storage/provider plan minimizing expected_cost + lambda*p95 + penalty(policy_violation) under deadline and privacy constraints
inventions.invariant: No cloud plan is production-ready unless compute provider, storage backend, retry policy, cost estimate, and data boundary are explicit.
build_steps: Add plan scoring over local, SSH, managed GPU, AWS, R2/S3, Supabase, and provider-hosted options.
Estimate wall-clock, queue time, storage egress, accelerator cost, and expected retry cost.
Attach policy filters for data residency, zero-retention, and BYOC-only modes.
Emit idempotent job specs with webhook and cancellation paths.
Record actual cost and latency after job completion for future calibration.
Expose plan explanations to CLI, TUI, account, and API.
acceptance_tests: A no-local-GPU training request gets a viable remote plan or precise missing-env diagnostic.
A BYOC-only request rejects public hosted GPU providers.
A storage-missing request blocks before uploading data.
Completed jobs feed observed cost and latency back into estimates.

inventions.name: Build Strategy Brain
inventions.objective: choose among prompt-only, RAG, route, fine-tune, distill, compile, quantize, local runtime, and cloud GPU paths by minimizing expected total cost subject to K-score confidence and enterprise policy constraints.
inventions.invariant: A recommended build path must carry the evidence chain: captured trace hash, data sufficiency estimate, privacy mode, compute plan, expected K-score interval, and fallback route.
build_steps: Represent every product path as a policy action with inputs, output artifact, privacy obligations, compute needs, and proof requirements.
Estimate data sufficiency, expected K-score interval, latency, cost, and operator burden for each action.
Use off-policy replay from captured traces to score routing, cache, RAG, distill, and compile alternatives before users spend GPU budget.
Apply enterprise constraints for zero retention, BYOC, region, model provider, and allowed runtime targets.
Emit a ranked plan with exact commands for CLI, TUI, account UI, and API wrappers.
Record why rejected paths failed so first-time users are not left guessing.
acceptance_tests: A low-data request recommends capture/eval before distillation and names the minimum missing examples.
A no-local-GPU request chooses cloud broker or hosted teacher options and emits storage/env diagnostics.
A strict zero-retention request rejects provider paths that cannot satisfy the privacy policy.
A mature workload receives a ranked build path with K-score interval, compute cost, fallback chain, and exact next command.
The same policy plan is visible from CLI JSON, TUI, account UI, and /v1 build-plan API.

inventions.name: Codegraph Risk Index
inventions.objective: rank files and product surfaces by expected failure impact times uncertainty, then require evidence before release.
inventions.invariant: A release gate cannot use stale codegraph evidence if touched files changed after graph generation.
build_steps: Extend codegraph nodes with product surface, route, CLI command, TUI view, package, and readiness requirement ownership.
Score changed files by dependency fanout, public exposure, missing tests, and stale verification age.
Require focused gates based on touched surface classes.
Emit release diagnostics that name exact proof commands.
Connect package release readiness and product graph into one risk report.
Keep frontend route failures separate from backend verifier failures.
acceptance_tests: Changing a package manifest requires package-release checks.
Changing router/OpenAPI files requires product surface and OpenAPI checks.
Changing public pages requires UI/static route checks.
Risk report includes stale evidence when graph timestamp predates modified files.
